data.title: Halma Consensus Upgrades Likely After Update — Market Talk
data.description: Halma can expect a slight 1%-2% consensus upgrade on the back of its update, UBS analysts Andre Kukhnin and Justin Wong say in a note. The safety, health and environmental-technology company continues to showcase strength and confirms good organic constant currency growth, the analysts say. The healthcare division has returned to organic growth and the safety unit continues to see very strong demand despite a tough construction backdrop, the analysts say. UBS moves up fiscal 2025 revenue estimates by 1% to 2.21 billion pounds, in line with consensus, and the adjusted EBIT estimate by 2% to 474 million pounds, slightly higher than consensus. Shares are up 4.4% at 2,755 pence.(anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

data.title: N. Macedonia's Pekabesko net profit up 19% y/y in 2024
data.description:
SKOPJE (N. Macedonia), March 13 (SeeNews) - North Macedonia-based meat processing company Pekabesko [MSE:PKB] said that it generated a net profit of 174.1 million denars ($3.08 million/2.8 million euro) in 2024, up 19% year-on-year.

Pekabesko's total operating revenue rose 8% on the year to 5.96 billion denars in 2024, as sales grew in both domestic and foreign markets, the company said in an annual financial statement on Wednesday.

Domestic sales added 7% to reach 5.05 billion denars in 2024, whereas sales from foreign markets increased 11% year-on-year to 832.3 million denars.

Total operating expenses were 7% higher at 5.77 billion denars in 2024, compared with the previous year.

Pekabesko's shares last traded on the Skopje bourse on August 26, at an average price of 17,550 denars.

(1 euro = 61.52 denars)

data.title: India's Ramco Cements eyes steepest weekly fall in over a year on tax hit
data.description:
** Indian cement makers Ramco Cements down 3% at 796.20 rupees, Dalmia Bharat down 2.2% at 1,605 rupees

** Ramco down ~8% so far this week, its steepest weekly fall since week ending February 5, 2024

** DALB sees worst week since December 16, 2024, down 5.3%

** Southern Indian state Tamil Nadu imposed 160 rupees per ton tax on limestone mining this week

** Kotak Institutional Equities says TRCE is worst impacted as it has 50% clinker capacity in Tamil Nadu, sees 10% impact on FY26 EBITDA

** DALB said it expects 1.30 billion rupees ($14.94 million) per year impact due to mining tax, plans to pass cost to customers

** TRCE down 17% YTD, while DALB down over 9% YTD

($1 = 87.0400 Indian rupees)

data.title: UK's Cab Payments slides on weak annual profit
data.description:
** Shares of British money transfer group Cab Payments Holdings down 6.7% to 50p

** CABP reports FY adjusted profit before tax of 21.3 mln pounds, down 64% YoY

** Posts FY volumes growth of 7% bln pounds to 37.2 bln pounds, with developed markets growing 13% and emerging markets falling 1%

** CAB’s exposure to volatile emerging market currencies, combined with central bank interventions and fluctuating aid flows, led to weaker take rates and lower EM volumes, analysts at Peel Hunt say in a research note

** In January, the company announced a plan to cut jobs by 20% to focus on investment in artificial intelligence and automation

** Says the previously announced cost action is underway to reflect a new strategy to reshape the organisation for greater efficiency

** As of last close, the stock is down ~28.53% YTD

Daily performance is calculated by reference to the previous day NAV/share and VNAS (Vietnam All Share Index). Calendar month-to-date performance is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS as of 28 Feb 2025. Calendar year-to-date performance is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS at the previous calendar year end (31 Dec 2024). 1-year performance is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS as of 13 Mar 2024. 3-year CAGR (compound annual growth rate) is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS as of 13 Mar 2022. 5-year CAGR is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS as of 13 Mar 2020. 10-year CAGR is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS as of 13 Mar 2015. 15-year CAGR is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS as of 13 Mar 2010. Since inception CAGR is calculated by reference to NAV/share as of 30 Jun 2006. VNAS is sourced from Bloomberg, which uses back-tested positions for VNAS for before 27 Jan 2014 as released by HoSE (Ho Chi Minh City Stock Exchange).

The Estimated Daily NAV stated in this announcement is based on estimated valuations and accordingly the Company accepts no responsibility for the accuracy of the Estimated Daily NAV figure given, and neither is any responsibility implied. data.title: Shaanxi Fenghuo Electronics Secures Regulatory Approval for Asset Acquisition, Fundraising
data.description:
Shaanxi Fenghuo Electronics received approval from the China Securities Regulatory Commission to issue shares for acquiring a 98.395% stake in Shaanxi Changling Electronic Technology.

The regulator also approved the issue of shares to raise up to 890 million yuan in supporting funds, according to a Thursday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

data.title: RCS - Grainger PLC - Grainger’s science-based target approved by SBTi
data.description:
RNS Number : 5602A Grainger PLC 13 March 2025  

13 March 2025

Grainger plc

("Grainger" or "the Company")

Grainger's science-based target approved by SBTi, reinforcing commitment to emissions reduction

Grainger plc, the UK's largest listed residential landlord and leader in the Build-to-Rent ("BTR") sector, is pleased to announce it has received approval from the Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi), for its near-term science-based greenhouse gas emissions reduction target.

Starting from the base year 2023, Grainger is committed to reducing its absolute scope 1 and 2 GHG emissions by 42% by 2030.* Additionally, the company will reduce scope 3 GHG emissions from capital goods and downstream leased assets by 51.6% per square meter of residential gross internal area over the same period. Grainger reduces operational emissions in its buildings through a series of actions: communal refurbishments, unit refurbishments, replacing gas heating systems, generating renewable energy through solar PV, purchasing renewable energy, and reducing development emissions by partnering with contractors and ensuring sustainable design material choices.

The Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) is a corporate climate action organisation that enables companies and financial institutions worldwide to play their part in combating the climate crisis. This recognition underscores Grainger's commitment to reducing greenhouse gas emissions from the development and operation of its buildings by 2030 and driving meaningful change within the industry.

Grainger has already delivered measurable results including achieving an 8% reduction in Scope 1-2 emissions between FY23 and FY24 and improving the energy efficiency of their PRS portfolio with 95% of properties rated EPC A-C. Investing in the energy efficiency of properties not only enhances Grainger's customers' well-being but also delivers them with significant savings on energy costs.

Charlotte Hopkinson, Head of Sustainability and CSR at Grainger, said:

"Receiving SBTi validation is a key milestone in our sustainability journey, confirming that our decarbonisation efforts are both ambitious and credible. This approval strengthens Grainger's position to make a meaningful impact in reducing emissions.

"We remain committed to implementing initiatives that will drive continued progress toward a sustainable, low-carbon future, ensuring we contribute to the broader global effort to combat climate change."

*The target boundary includes land-related emissions and removals from bioenergy.

ENDS

For further information, please contact:

Media

Ginny Pulbrook / Tom Huddart / Tilly Butcher, Camarco:                     +44 (0) 20 3757 4992 / 4991

Notes To Editors:

Grainger is  the UK's largest listed residential landlord and leader in Build to Rent. We are changing the way people rent in the UK by providing high quality, professionally managed rental homes. We are building homes where people want to live, creating spaces residents enjoy and making a positive impact in local communities.

This information is provided by Reach, the non-regulatory press release distribution service of RNS, part of the London Stock Exchange. data.title: Volkswagen 2024 Seat Cupra Rev EUR14.53B
Rheinmetall Surprises Positively With Update on JV With Leonardo — Market Talk
data.description: Rheinmetall surprised positively on Wednesday with its update on its joint venture with Italian aerospace and defense group Leonardo for the production of combat vehicles, BofA Securities analyst Benjamin Heelan says. Analysts weren't expecting a peak in deliveries by 2031, nor Rheinmetall's announcement of its plan to produce 272 main battle tanks under the joint venture, he says. Heelan expects this peak to boost Rheinmetall's Vehicle Solutions business in 2029-30, and foresees scope for about 14 billion euros in sales by the end of the decade. Rheinmetall is flat at 1,265 euros, while Leonardo is up 0.4% at 43.91 euros. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

data.title: Nvidia Stock Falls. What Could Stop a Full-Blown Rebound. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By George Glover

Nvidia stock was sliding ahead of Thursday's opening bell, suggesting it may be a while before the chip maker is able to claw back all its recent losses.

Shares fell 0.8% to $114.83 in premarket trading. Futures tracking the benchmark S&P 500 index were down 0.5%.

Nvidia has climbed 2.7% this week through Wednesday's close — but it's still down 14% in 2025, tumbling as U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs have triggered a shift away from the high-flying tech stocks that have powered the broader market higher over the past two years.

Don't expect a full-on rebound until those trade tensions start to ease.

Write to George Glover at george.glover@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Hesai Group Could Post Stronger Earnings This Year — Market Talk
data.description: Hesai Group's earnings could be stronger this year, given the sensor maker's determination to curb operating expenses, Daiwa analysts write in a note. China's mass-market car market may drive stronger demand for LiDAR products, further boosting the company's performance this year, they say. Daiwa forecasts shipments of 3 million units of advanced driver-assistance systems LiDAR sensors this year in China, mainly driven by mid- to high-end cars with price tags over 150,000 yuan. Meanwhile, the robotics sector could drive some upside surprise for Hesai, as LiDAR is necessary to help robots navigate and avoid obstacles when operating in a complicate space, the investment bank says. Daiwa maintains a buy rating for the stock, which last closed at $22.55. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: Vopak Plans Additional EUR1 Billion Infrastructure Investments
data.description:
Koninklijke Vopak plans to invest an additional 1 billion euros by 2030 to grow its gas and industrial infrastructure footprint.

The Dutch tank storage company said in a Thursday release that the majority of the investments are marked for gas infrastructure to cater to energy security and affordability. A significant amount will be allocated to growth markets including India and China.

Consequently, the total investments in gas and industrial infrastructure by 2030 are anticipated to amount 2 billion euros.

data.title: Volkswagen 2024 Volkswagen Commercial Vehicles Rev EUR15.13B
News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Thursday at 5 AM ET
data.description:
Consumer Angst Is Striking All Income Levels

Signs of weakness are showing up in spending on everything from basics to luxuries.

The Trump Effect Heads Abroad. European CEOs Are Getting Ousted.

European stocks have trailed U.S. peers for years. That's changing now, and the pressure is on CEOs.

U.S. Federal Deficit Exceeds $1 Trillion Over 5 Months

Data disclosed by the Treasury Department showed an adjusted gap between federal spending and revenue of $1.063 trillion for October through February.

Trump set to nominate Michelle Bowman as Fed's top bank supervisor

President Donald Trump is set to nominate Federal Reserve Governor Michelle Bowman to be the central bank's top bank supervisor, a White House official confirmed to MarketWatch.

The Stock Market Is Finally Rallying. It May Be a Head Fake.

A tame inflation report is welcome news, but markets remain worried about President Donald Trump's tariffs and trade war.

Bond rally fizzles despite cool CPI inflation reading. What that says about investors.

A fleeting reaction to a cooler-than-expected February inflation reading on Wednesday is a signal that investors are consumed by recession fears.

Inflation Cooled to 2.8% in February, Lower Than Expected

Inflation eased last month, but the data may offer less comfort to businesses, consumers and the Fed than it otherwise would as looming tariffs may keep prices rising.

Bank of Canada Cuts Policy Rate to 2.75% as Economy Faces 'New Crisis'

The central bank must balance the risk of tariff-fueled higher inflation and the damage to growth from U.S. trade policy, Gov. Tiff Macklem says.

Europe, Canada Hit Back at U.S. Steel Tariffs

The EU is targeting a range of American goods, including whiskey and motorcycles, with 50% tariffs, taking aim at politically sensitive products.

WTO's Trade Barometer Picks Up, Pointing to Resilient Flows

The World Trade Organization's trade barometer rose, but it warned increased purchases by those seeking to get ahead of threatened tariffs might exaggerate the health of the global trading system.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Thursday at 5 AM ET
data.description:
Intel Names Lip-Bu Tan as Chief Executive Officer

The former CEO of Cadence Design Systems, steps in as the semiconductor manufacturer has lost market share.

Couche-Tard Doubles Down on Friendly Takeover of 7-Eleven Owner

The Canadian owner of Circle K will continue its friendly pursuit of Seven & i, executives said, sounding confident that they can overcome antitrust issues that could block the prospective acquisition in the U.S.

Generali Operating Profit Rises on Higher Premiums

Assicurazioni Generali said its operating profit rose on the performance of all its units over 2024 gross written premiums that increased more than expected.

Adobe Stock Slides Despite Earnings Beat. The CFO Is Confident About AI.

Adobe posted first-quarter adjusted earnings of $5.08 a share, beating estimates of $4.97 a share.

LVMH Taps Arnault's Son to Head Loro Piana in Leadership Reshuffle

The fashion role is expected to bolster Frédéric Arnault's credentials as a candidate to one day lead the luxury-goods giant.

Puma Will Cut 500 Positions Globally as Part of a Cost-Cutting Plan

Job reductions will be implemented at the group's headquarters in Germany and in regional offices. The company has a total workforce of 22,500.

D-Wave Claims 'Quantum Supremacy,' Beating Traditional Computers

D-Wave is the latest quantum computing company to say it has achieved "quantum supremacy," better known as the ability to solve a problem with a quantum computer that can't be done with a traditional computer.

Niantic to Sell Pokemon GO, Other Games to Saudi-Backed Group in $3.5 Billion Deal

Niantic is also selling "Pikmin Bloom," and "Monster Hunter Now," Niantic's most recent game.

Sunnova Prepares Restructuring Talks That Could Include Bankruptcy Filing

Sunnova Energy, one of the country's largest residential solar companies, is preparing to engage with creditors to explore options including a potential bankruptcy filing over its roughly $8.5 billion in debt, according to people familiar with the matter.

Wonder Acquires Tastemade for Its Latest Quest: to Make a 'Mealtime Super App'

Wonder CEO Marc Lore hopes the company will one day offer AI-powered autonomous food ordering for all three meals of the day.

data.title: Kepler ups Novo, says risks and cuts to earnings priced in
data.description:
** Kepler Cheuvreux raises Novo Nordisk to "buy" from "hold" on priced in risks and cuts to longer-term earnings, saying it "is finally the entry point"

** Kepler says an uptick in the Wegovy drug prescriptions is "absolutely" implied by the Danish drugmaker's recent above-consensus 2025 outlook, adding it will be a key driver of sentiment near-term

** The broker sees potential for upside to Novo if competitor Eli Lilly's orforglipron GLP-1 tablet read-outs underwhelm on efficacy or safety

** Semaglutide's trial on people with Alzheimer's likely read-out in Q3 "has good risk-reward," says Kepler, adding there are several positive precedents or indicators that it may work, despite little forecasts or optimism for it

** The broker expects the REDEFINE-4 trial to show about 25% weight loss for CagriSema drug in late 2026, using more optimised dosing

** Out of 28 analysts covering the stock, 19 rate it "strong buy" or "buy," eight "hold," and one "strong sell," according to LSEG data

data.title: YCIC Eco-Technology Signs Supplementary Agreement for Five Projects
data.description:
YCIC Eco-Technology signed a supplementary agreement related to five landscaping and ecological projects awarded between 2022 and 2023, according to a Tuesday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

Under the supplementary agreement, YCIC Eco-Technology will pay Yunnan Transportation Planning & Design Institute a total of 12 million yuan in design fees for the five projects.

data.title: European truck shares fall on U.S. electric vehicle rules reversal
data.description:
Shares in European truck makers fell on Thursday, with traders and analysts pointing to the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency's (EPA) decision to start efforts to reverse the Biden administration's vehicle emissions rules.

Daimler Truck , which was contacted by Reuters but was not immediately available for comment, took the biggest hit and was down 8% at 0847 GMT.

The EPA said it is also reconsidering a 2022 regulation that aims to drastically cut smog- and soot-forming emissions from heavy-duty trucks, saying the rule makes trucks more expensive.

Analysts said trucking fleet firms had been expected to "pre-buy" trucks before the rules took effect, driving up demand, but this is now unlikely to happen.

The United States is the most important market for Daimler, which has invested heavily in emission-free drive systems with a view to climate protection goals and corresponding regulations.

Other big fallers in Europe include Sweden's Volvo and Traton , down 4%.
